title: T police release video of ‘anti-social, violent’ suspect on the Orange Line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA Transit Police released a video of a suspect kicking a train window on the Orange Line at Jackson Square.
The suspect allegedly assaulted a woman on the train on May 8, causing alarm among passengers.
Police are asking the public to help identify the man by contacting the Criminal Investigations Unit at 617-222-1050.

title: Man arrested after allegedly grabbing onto child, becoming ‘aggressive’ on Green Line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 33-year-old man was arrested after becoming aggressive and grabbing a child's leg on the Green Line.
The incident started when a woman declined to give her phone number, leading to unwanted overtures by the man.
Transit police captured the suspect near Chauncy/Summer Street; charges pending as investigation continues.

title: 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seven MassDOT employees were placed on leave following accusations of falsifying timesheets for overtime pay.
A Boston News 25 investigation revealed discrepancies between employees' reported work hours and observed behavior.
MassDOT stated it is taking allegations seriously and will hold accountable any misuse of taxpayer dollars.

title: Theo Epstein shares his thoughts on state of 2026 Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Theo Epstein reflects on the 2026 Red Sox's 22-27 start, emphasizing the need to boost their offensive production.
Epstein praises the team’s pitching and defense, highlighting these as solid foundations for improvement.
Injury setbacks like Roman Anthony's finger sprain hit the lineup, but scoring potential shown in recent victories.

title: Person who attacked Harvard student from behind snuck into MIT dorm shortly after, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A suspect attacked a Harvard student at Lowell House, attempting to force them into a room Tuesday evening.
The same suspect later entered MIT's Sidney-Pacific dorm, acting suspiciously and matching the initial attacker's description.
MIT police released an image of the suspect, described as a white male, 5'9", thin build, in a white t-shirt and dark shorts.

title: Police: Woman, 85, found dead in R.I. hotel after son dies by suicide
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Terry Costello, 85, was found dead in a Rhode Island hotel room, leading to a homicide investigation.
Police were attempting to notify Costello of her son’s suicide when they discovered her body.
Authorities consider Costello's death isolated, posing no active threat to the public, pending autopsy results.

title: If Bruins are looking to make a deal, the Ducks are an appealing trade partner
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are considering Ducks' players Olen Zellweger and Mason McTavish as potential summer trade targets.
Anaheim Ducks may be open to trades to manage cap space and redundancies after playoff success.
Zellweger offers defensive speed upside, while McTavish could fill a top-six center role for Bruins.

title: Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A porbeagle shark was found stranded and later died in Point Judith Pond near Narragansett, Rhode Island.
The Atlantic Shark Institute documented the shark sightings, which began at Salty Brine State Beach and ended in Billington Cove.
Porbeagle sharks are common in New England due to their preference for cooler waters, but this location was unusual.

title: Families continue to fight BPS exam school admissions policy after judge rules no discrimination against white, Asian students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Boston Parent Coalition for Academic Excellence filed an appeal against Boston's tiered admissions process for exam schools.
The appeal argues the socioeconomic tiers in school admissions act as a proxy for race, affecting white and Asian students.
A federal judge dismissed the original lawsuit, stating the policies don't violate equal protection under the Fourteenth Amendment.

title: Patriots receivers well aware of the A.J. Brown trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots linked to a potential trade for Eagles' receiver A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
Patriots receiver Kyle Williams and safety Kevin Byard acknowledge trade rumors, praising Brown's skill and career achievements.
Eagles face a $43.3 million dead cap hit if Brown is traded before June 1, reducing to $16.3 million afterward.
